CertInfo.description:
One certificate (or CA) and its expiry (agent push only).

Parsed from ``config.xml`` <cert>/<ca> via ``openssl``. ``days_remaining`` may
be negative for an already-expired certificate.
CertInfo.title: CertInfo

CommentSet.description: Upsert payload. An empty/whitespace ``comment`` deletes the entry.
CommentSet.title: CommentSet

ConfigInfo.description: Last config-change metadata from ``config.xml`` <revision> (agent push only).
ConfigInfo.title: ConfigInfo

ConfigInfoResponse.description:
Last config change (from the agent's config.xml <revision>) plus the last
time a config backup was downloaded through the dashboard (from the audit log).
ConfigInfoResponse.title: ConfigInfoResponse

ConnMonitorState.description: A monitor joined with its latest pushed ping result (for the status view).
ConnMonitorState.title: ConnMonitorState

ConnMonitorUpdate.description: Partial update — only provided fields are applied.
ConnMonitorUpdate.title: ConnMonitorUpdate

ConnPingTestRequest.description: Ad-hoc ping test from the config dialog (not persisted).
ConnPingTestRequest.title: ConnPingTestRequest

CpuUsage.description:
GET /api/diagnostics/cpu_usage/getCPUType or stream endpoint.

OPNsense returns various shapes depending on version; we normalize to a
single total percentage.
CpuUsage.title: CpuUsage

DeviceType.description: What kind of device an instance represents.
DeviceType.title: DeviceType

DiagnosisSection.description: One titled block of readable diagnostic text (config, SA state, log, …).
DiagnosisSection.title: DiagnosisSection

ExternalIpInfo.description:
Public IP(s) + NAT signal for the network tab.

``ipv4``/``ipv6`` are the agent's ipify probe (last known, sticky). ``source_ip``
is the peer IP the hub saw on the agent's WS connect (None when disconnected).
``behind_nat`` is True when the box's public IPv4 is not one of its own
interface addresses — a NAT gateway owns the public address.
ExternalIpInfo.title: ExternalIpInfo

FirmwareUpgradeStatus.description: Progress while an upgrade is running.
FirmwareUpgradeStatus.title: FirmwareUpgradeStatus

GroupBrief.description: Minimal group reference embedded in user payloads (/auth/me, /users).
GroupBrief.title: GroupBrief

GroupInstanceOut.description:
Instance reference for the move-UI — deliberately no status/config data,
so a pure superadmin can reassign instances without instance access.
GroupInstanceOut.title: GroupInstanceOut

IPsecChild.description:
One Phase-2 entry (child SA) of a tunnel, plus its optional ping result.

``state`` is the live child SA state (INSTALLED / REKEYING / …) or "" when the
Phase 2 is configured but down. The ``ping_*`` fields are populated only when a
ping monitor is configured for this child (else ``ping_state`` stays "none").
IPsecChild.title: IPsecChild

IPsecDiagnosis.description:
Readable diagnostic bundle for one tunnel — for the human and for pasting
into an LLM. Raw text per section, deliberately not over-parsed.
IPsecDiagnosis.title: IPsecDiagnosis

LoadAvg.description:
System load average over 1/5/15 minutes (agent push only). ``cores==0`` means
no data (direct poll, or a pre-1.8.1 agent) → the load check skips, since load
is only meaningful normalised per CPU core.
LoadAvg.title: LoadAvg

LoginChallenge.description:
Step-1 result.

``stage`` is ``"verify"`` when the account already has a factor enrolled,
``"enroll"`` when mandatory 2FA setup must happen first, or ``"done"`` when the
session is already minted — the password-only bootstrap admin, which is exempt
from 2FA. On ``"done"`` the user is attached.
LoginChallenge.title: LoginChallenge

NtpStatus.description:
NTP sync state (agent push only). ``stratum==-1`` means no data; ``stratum==16``
is a reachable-but-unsynced clock (a soft state, never CRIT).
NtpStatus.title: NtpStatus

PfStatus.description: pf state-table usage (agent push only). ``states_limit==0`` means no data.
PfStatus.title: PfStatus

PfTopFlow.description: One individual state (flow) ranked into the top list by lifetime bytes.
PfTopFlow.title: PfTopFlow

PfTopSummary.description:
On-box aggregation of ``pfctl -vss`` (agent push only, ~5-min cadence):
top talkers, states per interface/protocol and the biggest flows — traffic
insight without NetFlow. ``ts`` is when the agent walked the state table.
PfTopSummary.title: PfTopSummary

PfTopTalker.description:
One aggregated talker (source or destination IP) from the pf state table.
``bytes`` is the in+out total over each state's lifetime — not a rate.
PfTopTalker.title: PfTopTalker

PingMonitorUpdate.description: Partial update — only provided fields are applied.
PingMonitorUpdate.title: PingMonitorUpdate

PingTestRequest.description: Ad-hoc ping test from the config dialog (not persisted).
PingTestRequest.title: PingTestRequest

ServiceAlert.description:
A ServiceCheck with its instance and Checkmk export selection status.

Used by the /checks aggregate (Alerts page) so consumers see every evaluated
check plus whether it is currently exported to Checkmk (opt-in, base default
off).
ServiceAlert.title: ServiceAlert

ServiceCheck.description: One evaluated service for one instance.
ServiceCheck.title: ServiceCheck

ServiceInfo.description:
One system service and whether it is running (agent push only).

OPNsense: ``configctl service list``; pfSense: ``get_services()`` via PHP.
ServiceInfo.title: ServiceInfo

SystemStatus.description: Aggregated poll snapshot for one instance.
SystemStatus.title: SystemStatus

Transport.description: How the dashboard reaches a device.
Transport.title: Transport

get.description:
Aggregated timeline (Logs-page pattern): one row per recurring event with
count + last seen, instead of one row per occurrence.

schema.title: Response Access Grouped Api Access Log Grouped Get

post.description:
Public: exchange a valid one-time code for an instance's agent token.

Unauthenticated attack surface → rate-limited per IP with the login limiter.
The code must be unused and unexpired; it is consumed on success.

get.description: The caller's visible connected agents, annotated with update availability.

schema.title: Response List Connected Agents Api Agents Connected Get

post.description:
Push the container's agent code to every connected agent that is out of date.

Up-to-date agents are skipped so they are not needlessly restarted.

schema.title: Response Update All Agents Api Agents Update All Post
Response List Apikeys Api Apikeys Get
Response Revoke Apikey Api Apikeys  Key Id  Delete

delete.description:
Hard-delete a key row. Only a revoked key can be purged — an active key
must be revoked first (revoke = soft, drops its recoverable copy; purge =
remove the row entirely so it stops cluttering the list).

schema.title: Response Delete Apikey Api Apikeys  Key Id  Purge Delete

get.description:
Return the full token of a revealable, non-revoked key (audited).

Same 404 for missing / revoked / not-revealable / out-of-binding — no oracle.

parameters.description: Filter by action prefix, e.g. 'auth' or 'ipsec.connect'
parameters.name: action

schema.description: Filter by action prefix, e.g. 'auth' or 'ipsec.connect'
schema.title: Action

parameters.description: Only show entries from the last N hours
parameters.name: hours

schema.description: Only show entries from the last N hours
schema.title: Hours

post.description:
Step 1 of login: verify the password, then hand off to the second factor.

A correct password never mints a session on its own — it stores a *pending*
state and returns a challenge. The session is minted only by the matching
``/auth/mfa/*`` endpoint once TOTP or a passkey passes.
Finish enrollment: verify the first code, enable TOTP, mint the session.

delete.description: Remove a passkey — but never the account's last remaining second factor.

post.description:
Generate a fresh TOTP secret for enrollment (not yet enabled).

The secret is stored encrypted with ``totp_enabled`` still False — it only
becomes a usable factor after ``/confirm/totp`` echoes a valid code.
Login step 2 for an already-enrolled user.
Login step 2 with a passkey assertion.
Add a passkey to the signed-in account (no new session).
Finish passkey enrollment, then mint the session.

Run an action on multiple instances in parallel.

Caller-supplied ids outside the user's groups are silently dropped by the
scope filter — never acted on, never confirmed to exist.

Trigger a bounded tcpdump via the agent.

payload: {
  "interface": "igb0",
  "filter": "host 10.0.0.5 and port 443",  # optional BPF
  "max_seconds": 30,
  "max_bytes": 1000000
}
Returns {capture_id, meta} on success. Use the id for download/view.

schema.title: Response Start Packet Capture Api Captures Instances  Instance Id  Post

get.description: Return parsed packet summary for the in-browser viewer + meta.

schema.title: Response Get Packets Api Captures  Cap Id  Packets Get

get.description: Download the raw pcap. Authenticated users can fetch by id (id is opaque).

Fleet-wide certificate inventory across the caller's visible instances.

Certs are agent-push only (parsed from config.xml on the box), so direct-poll
and Securepoint instances contribute nothing. Pure in-memory hub read — no
appliance round-trips. Sorted soonest-expiry-first so the timeline reads top-down.

All evaluated service checks across the caller's visible instances (the
data Checkmk receives; API keys honor their group binding, unbound = global).

Each entry is annotated with whether it is currently exported to Checkmk. The
export is opt-in (base default off): ``excluded`` is true for any check no
selection rule has included. The Alerts page consumes this — and auto-
refetches every 30s, which is machine cadence: direct-poll instances serve
the shared stale-while-revalidate cache (prod incident: every page refresh
live-polled the Securepoint appliances at 1.3s+ per request).

schema.title: Response All Checks Api Checks Get

get.description:
All comments across the caller's visible instances (optionally one kind).

Overview pages (VPN, connectivity, firmware, certs) merge these client-side by
(instance_id, entity_key). Out-of-scope rows drop out via the visible-id set —
a zero-group user therefore sees none.

schema.title: Response Comments Overview Api Comments Get

get.description:
Every standalone connectivity monitor across the caller's visible
instances, joined with its latest pushed ping state from the agent-hub
cache. Pure DB + in-memory — no appliance round-trips (the firewalls do the
pinging, the agent pushes results). Monitors of out-of-scope instances drop
out via the ``by_id`` lookup below.

All visible instances' checks in one call — consumed by the Checkmk special agent.

API-key callers honor the key's group binding (unbound = global); a session
user only gets their groups' instances.

Push instances use the hub cache (cheap); direct instances are polled live but
cached for a short TTL, shared with the Prometheus export so the two integrations
don't each poll the same appliance.

schema.title: Response Export Checkmk Api Export Checkmk Get

get.description: Export the caller's visible instances as CSV.

All visible instances' checks in Prometheus text format (Grafana-ready scrape).

Same auth and group scoping as the Checkmk export: API-key callers honor the
key's group binding (unbound = global), session users get their groups'
instances. Unlike Checkmk there is no selection filtering, no aggregation and
no blackout — every evaluated check becomes a series; filter in PromQL. Push
instances read the hub cache (cheap); direct instances are polled live but
cached for a short TTL so frequent scrapes don't hammer the appliances.
Fetch firmware status from the caller's visible instances in parallel.

schema.title: Response Refresh Db Now Api Geoip Db Refresh Post

get.description:
Persistent denial statistics (stats aggregate + sampled recent events,
migration 040); not-yet-flushed denials are folded in live.

schema.title: Response Geoip Denials Api Geoip Denials Get

get.description:
Global denied-request count, visible to EVERY logged-in user (footer).

Deliberately just the aggregate number from the in-memory mirror (no DB
read, no IPs/countries/paths — those stay superadmin-only in /denials).

schema.title: Response Geoip Denials Summary Api Geoip Denials Summary Get
Response List Groups Api Groups Get
Response List Group Channels Api Groups  Group Id  Channels Get
Response List Group Instances Api Groups  Group Id  Instances Get

get.description:
forward_auth target: 200 only if the orbit_gui cookie is valid for THIS instance.

Zero-I/O (HMAC verify only) — runs on every asset. The origin's instance comes
from the `instance` query (Caddy per-port dev) or the `gui-<id>` Host (Traefik
wildcard prod). The cookie's instance must equal it — a cookie minted for one
firewall can't satisfy another's gate (cross-tenant defense).

schema.title: Response Gui Authcheck Api Gui Authcheck Get

get.description: Exchange a valid handoff token for an origin-scoped orbit_gui cookie (via Caddy).
Returns 200 if DB is reachable, 503 otherwise. Includes app + DB schema versions.
Hub health right now — all numbers are in-memory since ``started_at``.

schema.title: Response List All Api Instances Get

get.description:
Global interval defaults so the UI can show what an empty override inherits.

Declared before ``/{instance_id}`` so the literal path wins the match.

schema.title: Response Interval Defaults Api Instances Defaults Get

post.description: Send a command to a connected agent.

schema.title: Response Send Agent Command Api Instances  Instance Id  Agent Command Post

post.description: Disable agent mode, revoke token, fall back to polling.

schema.title: Response Disable Agent Api Instances  Instance Id  Agent Disable Post

post.description: Enable agent mode for an instance. Generates a unique token.

Mint a one-time, 1-hour enrollment code for an instance (admin only).

The agent trades it at /agent/enroll for this instance's token, so the token
never has to be pasted by hand. Only the code's SHA-256 is stored.
Ask the agent to drop and re-establish its dashboard WebSocket.

schema.title: Response Reconnect Agent Api Instances  Instance Id  Agent Reconnect Post

post.description:
Force the agent to re-collect its interval-gated metrics now.

logfiles (hourly), firmware (~12h) and the config backup are normally
throttled; this pushes a fresh full snapshot on demand (e.g. to heal a
Log-Events view after a fix, instead of waiting for the next hourly tick).

schema.title: Response Refresh Agent Api Instances  Instance Id  Agent Refresh Post

get.description:
Return the stored agent token for an instance (agent mode must be enabled).

Write-gated (not just authenticated): the token is a bearer credential to the
agent WebSocket — anyone holding it can evict the real agent and push forged
metrics — so a read-only session must not be able to read it.

schema.title: Response Get Agent Token Api Instances  Instance Id  Agent Token Get

post.description:
Tell the connected agent to remove itself, then drop agent mode.

The agent acks and a detached script tears it down (supervisor first); on a
successful ack we revoke the token and fall back to direct transport so the
dashboard stops expecting a push agent.

schema.title: Response Uninstall Agent Api Instances  Instance Id  Agent Uninstall Post

post.description:
Push the container's agent code to one connected agent (self-update).

Per-instance by design: this is the canary mechanism (DR-6). Update one
instance, confirm it reconnects healthy at the new version, then the next.

schema.title: Response Update Agent Api Instances  Instance Id  Agent Update Post

get.description: Certificates and their expiry (agent push only). Direct/Securepoint = empty.

schema.title: Response Certificates Api Instances  Instance Id  Certificates Get

get.description: Evaluated OK/WARN/CRIT checks for one instance (memory, disks, gateways, IPsec, firmware).

schema.title: Response Instance Checks Api Instances  Instance Id  Checks Get

get.description:
Recorded check state-change history for one instance, most recent first.

``key`` exact-matches one entity (``connectivity:5``, ``availability``);
``key_prefix`` matches a whole category (``gateway:``). Populated by the
agent-push ingest (plus availability from the scheduler); direct-API instances
have only availability history.

schema.title: Response Instance Check History Api Instances  Instance Id  Checks History Get

get.description: All comments of one instance (every kind — per-instance tabs filter).

schema.title: Response Instance Comments Api Instances  Instance Id  Comments Get

put.description: Upsert one comment; an empty/whitespace comment deletes the entry.

schema.title: Response Set Comment Api Instances  Instance Id  Comments Put

get.description: Download OPNsense config.xml. Agent mode: request via agent command.

schema.title: Response List Instance Config Backups Api Instances  Instance Id  Config Backups Get

get.description: Unified diff between two stored versions ("what changed on this box?").
Download one stored version as XML (disaster recovery).
Config-change metadata + last backup time. Revision is agent-push only.
List the configured connectivity ping monitors for an instance.

schema.title: Response List Monitors Api Instances  Instance Id  Connectivity Monitors Get

post.description: Create a connectivity monitor and push the updated set to the agent.

Run a one-off ping via the agent so the user can validate source/dest before saving.

Agent-mode only — the probe runs on the firewall (a direct-mode instance has
no agent to ping from).

delete.description: Delete a connectivity monitor and push the updated set to the agent.

patch.description: Update a connectivity monitor and push the updated set to the agent.

get.description:
Each configured monitor joined with its latest pushed ping result.

The live result comes from the agent-hub cache, keyed by monitor id; a monitor
with no result yet (just added, or agent hasn't pushed) reports ``none``.

schema.title: Response Connectivity Status Api Instances  Instance Id  Connectivity Status Get

get.description:
The box's public IPv4/IPv6 + a NAT indicator (agent mode).

Composes the agent's ipify probe (hub cache) with the source IP the hub saw on
connect and derives ``behind_nat``. Out-of-scope/missing instances 404 (no
existence oracle) — same scope gate as ``/status``.
Fetch recent firewall log entries. Agent mode: return cached push data.

schema.title: Response Firewall Log Api Instances  Instance Id  Firewall Log Get

get.description:
Return alias name + address pairs for Source/Dest alias completion.

OPNsense uses its alias API (``alias/search_item``, reachable directly or
tunneled through a push agent's relay). pfSense has no such API, so its push
agent parses ``config.xml`` and answers the ``get_aliases`` command. An empty
but successful agent reply must not mask the API path, so the agent is only
used when it actually returns entries.

schema.title: Response Get Aliases Api Instances  Instance Id  Firewall Aliases Get

get.description: Get firmware status. Agent mode: return last push from the agent.

post.description: Trigger a firmware update check. Agent mode: send command to agent.
Trigger firmware update. Agent mode: send command to agent.

Start the vendor series/major upgrade (e.g. OPNsense 26.1 → 26.7).

Agent mode only: the agent resolves the target from the box's own
unlocked upgrade path (a request-supplied target is deliberately not
honored) and creates a ZFS boot environment first. The action string is
in _INTERNAL_AGENT_ACTIONS so the generic command passthrough rejects it.
Direct-poll instances keep using the vendor GUI (501).

get.description:
Poll upgrade progress.

Agent mode: ask the agent. linux answers running/done from apt/dpkg
process state + dpkg log tail (§25); OPNsense/pfSense (agent >= 3.0.4)
from the vendor updater's progress log + process state. Agents predating
the command answer unknown-action → degrade to the historic "unknown"
(the UI stops tracking after a timeout).

schema.title: Response Gateways Api Instances  Instance Id  Gateways Get

put.description:
Move an instance to another group.

Superadmin: any instance to any group. Role admin: member of BOTH source
and target group. A dedicated endpoint (not part of the generic PATCH)
because moving is a rights operation, not instance config.

post.description: Mint a short-lived handoff URL that logs the browser into the GUI proxy origin.

parameters.description: Deep-link path inside the GUI, e.g. /ui/ipsec/sessions
parameters.name: path

schema.description: Deep-link path inside the GUI, e.g. /ui/ipsec/sessions
schema.title: Path

get.description: List all IPsec tunnels with status. Agent mode: return cached push data.

post.description: Connect a single IPsec tunnel. Agent mode: send command to agent.
Disconnect a single IPsec tunnel. Agent mode: send command to agent.

get.description: List the configured Phase-2 ping monitors for an instance.

schema.title: Response List Ping Monitors Api Instances  Instance Id  Ipsec Ping Monitors Get

post.description: Create a Phase-2 ping monitor and push the updated set to the agent.

Run a one-off ping via the agent so the user can validate source/dest before saving.

Agent-mode only — the probe runs on the firewall (a direct-mode instance has
no agent to ping from).

delete.description: Delete a Phase-2 ping monitor and push the updated set to the agent.

patch.description: Update a Phase-2 ping monitor and push the updated set to the agent.

post.description:
Re-check now instead of waiting for the next poll/push cycle.

Agent mode: ask the agent to collect and push a fresh snapshot
(``status.refresh``). The hub ingests frames in order, so the metrics push
lands before the command ack — the cache read below is already fresh.
Agents predating the command answer unknown-action; degrade to last-known
data rather than failing the re-check. Direct mode: a plain live fetch,
kept on this endpoint so the UI has one uniform call.
Restart the IPsec service. Agent mode: send command to agent.

get.description:
Readable diagnostic bundle for one tunnel (config, SAs, IPsec log, peer ping).

Agent mode: the agent gathers on-box (swanctl + strongSwan log + ping) and
pushes the result over its WebSocket. Direct mode: Securepoint gathers via SSH.

Recorded state-change history for one tunnel, most recent first.

Populated by the agent-push ingest (push mode); direct-API instances have no
history yet and return an empty list.

schema.title: Response Ipsec Tunnel History Api Instances  Instance Id  Ipsec  Tunnel Id  History Get
Response List Instance Logs Api Instances  Instance Id  Logs Get

parameters.description: Differentiate a counter into a per-second rate
parameters.name: rate

schema.description: Differentiate a counter into a per-second rate
schema.title: Rate
Response Pf Top Api Instances  Instance Id  Pf Top Get

post.description:
Enable the local API relay on a connected agent (idempotent).

On pfSense this installs the community REST API package (needs internet egress
on the firewall) and provisions the relay user; on OPNsense it just provisions.
Explicit by design — never an automatic side-effect of first relay use.

schema.title: Response Enable Relay Api Instances  Instance Id  Relay Enable Post

post.description:
Make a real authenticated API call to the firewall through the agent relay.

Picks a platform-appropriate API endpoint and reports whether it answered 2xx
(relay + credentials work), plus round-trip latency. Runs server-side so a
relayed 401/403 never reaches the browser — that would otherwise trip the
auto-logout in the API wrapper.

delete.description: Proxy ``{method} /relay/<path>`` to the firewall's local API via its agent.

get.description: Proxy ``{method} /relay/<path>`` to the firewall's local API via its agent.

patch.description: Proxy ``{method} /relay/<path>`` to the firewall's local API via its agent.

post.description: Proxy ``{method} /relay/<path>`` to the firewall's local API via its agent.

put.description: Proxy ``{method} /relay/<path>`` to the firewall's local API via its agent.

get.description:
Per-service running state. Agent mode: last push from the hub cache.
Direct/Securepoint poll does not carry services → empty list.

schema.title: Response Services Api Instances  Instance Id  Services Get

get.description:
Current status snapshot.

- Agent mode: return the last push received from the agent (in-memory cache).
  Falls back to an empty SystemStatus if the agent hasn't connected yet.
- Polling mode: call poll_status() on demand for a near-real-time view.

post.description: Show exactly what anonymized text would leave the box before sending it.

schema.title: Response List Providers Api Llm Providers Get
Response Global Log Events Api Logs Events Get

get.description: KPI tiles over the caller's visible instances (US-3.4).

The selectable categories (with whether each is globally included) plus every
rule. The frontend re-implements ``resolve`` against ``rules`` so toggling only
refetches this cheap config, not the live preview.

Live view of every instance's checks annotated with this consumer's resolved
on/off. Reads rules fresh from the DB (correct under multiple workers). Polls
direct-mode instances live (same caveat as the export): slow with many.

delete.description: Remove a rule (query params) — the check falls back to inherit / base default.

schema.title: Response Delete Rule Api Selection  Consumer  Rules Delete

post.description: Upsert an include/exclude rule (idempotent).
Send a test notification on a channel (ignoring selection). Channels only.

schema.title: Response Test Channel Api Selection  Consumer  Test Post
Response List Settings Api Settings Get

post.description: Restart the backend process (applies "needs restart" settings).

schema.title: Response Restart Backend Api Settings Restart Post
Response List Users Api Users Get

post.description:
Clear a user's second factor (recovery for a lost authenticator/passkey).

Wipes TOTP + all passkeys and bumps ``password_version`` so any live session
dies; the user is forced to re-enroll 2FA on their next login.

get.description: Fetch IPsec tunnels from the caller's visible instances in parallel.
